{"query":"Bukele MS-13 extraditions blocked ProPublica report 2025 U.S. investigation","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1765,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.propublica.org/article/bukele-trump-el-salvador-ms13-gang-vulcan-corruption-investigation","title":"How El Salvador’s Government Impeded a U.S. Probe of MS-13 — ProPublica","content":"Investigation Impeded: Despite Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele’s crime fighter reputation, his top aides blocked extraditions of MS-13 leaders to the U.S., officials say.
In striking the deal with the Salvadoran president, Trump has effectively undercut the Vulcan investigation and shielded Bukele from further scrutiny, current and former U.S. officials said.
Despite his government’s refusal to extradite gang bosses to the United States, the Trump administration in March deported one MS-13 leader accused of terrorism.
Rosen when he announced “the highest-reaching and most sweeping indictment targeting MS-13 and its command and control structure in U.S. history.”
He was the first MS-13 leader whose extradition was blocked.
Since Trump took office this year, his administration has redirected Vulcan’s mission to also target Tren de Aragua, a Venezuelan gang that the president has put in the spotlight."},{"url":"https://www.propublica.org/article/ambassador-ronald-johnson-nayib-bukele-trump-el-salvador","title":"How a U.S. Ambassador Allegedly Shielded President Bukele From Law Enforcement — ProPublica","content":"A Useful Friendship: A previously undisclosed report alleges Salvadoran President Bukele asked Trump’s ambassador to dismiss an embassy contractor helping U.S. agents investigate top aides.
In December 2021, the Treasury Department issued more sanctions against Luna and Marroquin, alleging that the Bukele aides negotiated the secret agreement with the MS-13 gang."},{"url":"https://www.latinamericareports.com/trumps-former-ambassador-shielded-bukele-and-eased-pressure-on-ms-13-pact-propublica/12599/","title":"Trump’s former ambassador shielded Bukele and eased pressure on MS-13 pact: ProPublica - Latin America Reports","content":"Trump’s former ambassador shielded Bukele and eased pressure on MS-13 pact: ProPublica
A ProPublica investigation has revealed that Ronald Douglas Johnson, the U.S. ambassador to El Salvador during Donald Trump’s first administration, favored the government of Nayib Bukele by softening pressure to extradite MS-13 members to the United States and by promoting the Salvadoran president’s image in Washington, thereby consolidating the relationship between the two leaders.
According to the investigation, published on September 30, with Johnson’s arrival, the U.S. embassy became a promoter of Bukele’s image among Republicans and the Trump administration.
Then the station chief would go see him and say the opposite,” said Juan Sebastián González, a former senior director for Western Hemisphere Affairs at the National Security Council, who received frequent reports about the embassy according to ProPublica.
His defenders, who highlighted his background as a military veteran and former CIA agent, argued that Johnson’s strategy sought to maintain a close relationship with Bukele and, in that way, advance the objectives of U.S. policy: reducing immigration."},{"url":"https://www.worldpoliticsreview.com/el-salvador-trump-investigation/","title":"Bukele’s MS-13 Deals Could Be a Problem for Trump, Too | WPR","content":"An investigation by ProPublica published today revealed that Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele’s administration cut a deal with leaders of the MS-13 gang in the early years of his presidency.
According to U.S. and Salvadoran officials, his government then impeded a U.S. task force investigating the deal, prevented the extradition of gang leaders that could have served as witnesses to the negotiations and made an agreement with U.S."}]}

The U.S. also sent back accused members of the notorious Salvadoran gang MS-13 — which both the U.S. and El Salvador have designated as a terrorist organization. New reporting on that investigation by ProPublica shows that senior officials in Bukele’s government repeatedly impeded the work of a U.S. task force as it pursued evidence of possible wrongdoing by the Salvadoran president and his inner circle. Bukele’s allies secretly blocked extraditions of gang leaders whom U.S. agents viewed as potential witnesses to the negotiations and persecuted Salvadoran law enforcement officials who helped the task force, according to exclusive interviews with current and former U.S. and Salvadoran officials, newly obtained internal documents and court records from both countries.","score":0.7644879,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qQZR7vPADEA","title":"Top Bukele aides blocked U.S. extraditions of MS-13 leaders ...","content":"New reporting from ProPublica finds Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele's top aides blocked the extraditions of MS-13 leaders to the U.S.","score":0.6854659,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.latintimes.com/el-salvador-president-trump-ally-bukele-accused-cutting-deals-ms-13-gang-members-using-us-aid-584958","title":"El Salvador President and Trump Ally Bukele Accused of ...","content":"According to ProPublica, Bukele's allies blocked extraditions of gang leaders whom U.S. agents viewed as potential witnesses to the negotiations","score":0.5855047,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.threads.com/@propublica/post/DKzVLvvxjX6/11-bukele-aides-carried-out-a-secret-campaign-to-block-the-extraditionsbukele-an","title":"11/ Bukele aides carried out a secret campaign to block the ...","content":"Bukele aides carried out a secret campaign to block the extraditions. a new ProPublica investigation found there's more to the story. funds to","score":0.56258565,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reddit.com/r/law/comments/1l9sbil/how_el_salvador_blocked_a_us_investigation_into","title":"How El Salvador blocked a U.S. investigation into MS-13 ...","content":"El Salvador blocked a U.S. investigation into MS-13 and corruption.
